what_it_does: Taste.tsx renders a small control panel with three toggle rows: a type theme (source/crimson/lora), a pigment theme (muted/soft/present), and a motion feel (spring/eased). It stores type and pigment in local state, sets corresponding data-type and data-pigment attributes on document.documentElement, and delegates the motion feel value through props.
observations: Uses local useState for type and pigment but receives feel/setFeel as controlled props for motion.
Directly mutates document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-type', id) and data-pigment in click handlers.
Includes an S4 slice-only scaffold comment saying taste calls are resolved by flipping and watching, not by speccing.
Uses a stamp('ui://controls/taste') spread on the root div.
ToggleRow is a private helper component that renders a label glyph and pill buttons.
The selected pill is styled via a pill--on class; there is no ARIA selected/pressed state.
Initial type is 'source' and initial pigment is 'soft', hardcoded in state.
Compact mode appends a taste--compact class.
standouts: The 'taste' metaphor for live type/pigment/motion exploration.
Direct DOM attribute mutation as the theming mechanism.
The S4 comment framing the component as an intentional scaffold for empirical design decisions.
conventions: React functional component with TypeScript props interface.
BEM-ish class naming (taste, taste--compact, toggle-row, toggle-pills, pill, pill--on).
Local state for UI-only selections and controlled props for externally meaningful state.
Imports a type from a sibling tokens module and a utility from lib/address.
concerns: DOM attribute and local state can drift if data-type/data-pigment are changed elsewhere.
No aria-pressed or aria-selected on the active pill, so screen readers may not perceive the current choice.
Initial type/pigment are not read from the existing document element, so the UI may not reflect the current theme on mount.
Direct document access in handlers is client-only; the component assumes a browser environment.
notes: A compact, interactive theme-and-motion sampler that drives live CSS through root data attributes while leaving motion feel to its parent.
questions: How should the initial type and pigment be derived if the document already has data-type/data-pigment set?
Should the active pill expose aria-pressed or role='radio' semantics?
What is the contract of stamp('ui://controls/taste') and is it required for telemetry or testing?
Is MotionFeel limited to exactly 'spring' and 'eased', or can it accept other feels?
purpose_vs_actual: The comment frames the file as a slice-only scaffold for resolving taste by flipping and watching; the code indeed implements a working flip-based selector for type, pigment, and motion, so it matches the stated scaffold purpose.
apparent_themes: Empirical design tuning by live flipping rather than upfront specification.
Runtime theming through CSS data attributes.
Separation of local presentational state from externally controlled motion state.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to give designers or users a quick, low-ceremony way to experiment with typeface, color pigment, and motion feel, treating the UI itself as the spec.
novelty: The 'taste' abstraction bundles typography, color weight, and motion into a single exploratory control, and it intentionally avoids over-specification by letting users flip values and observe the result.
contribution: A compact, reusable taste selector that maps user choices to runtime DOM theme attributes and delegates motion feel to a parent, making it easy to drop into any surface view for live design-token exploration.
summary_for_embedding: Taste.tsx is a React toggle panel that lets users switch document type and pigment themes via root data attributes and choose a motion feel, using an internal ToggleRow button group.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a quick, interactive 'taste' control for experimenting with typeface, color pigment, and motion feel in the surface app.

symbols.does: Renders three toggle rows for type, pigment, and motion feel, updating root data attributes and external motion state.
symbols.description: The exported Taste component holds local state for type and pigment, receives controlled motion feel props, and renders ToggleRow groups; it mutates document.documentElement data-type and data-pigment directly when selections change.

symbols.does: Updates the selected type state and writes the data-type attribute to the root element.
symbols.description: Event handler that synchronizes React state for the active type with a DOM-side theme attribute so CSS can re-tint/re-type live.

symbols.does: Updates the selected pigment state and writes the data-pigment attribute to the root element.
symbols.description: Event handler that synchronizes React state for the active pigment with a DOM-side theme attribute.

symbols.does: Renders a label and a row of pill buttons for picking one option.
symbols.description: Internal helper component that maps an options array to buttons, highlighting the active value with a pill--on class.
